must_detect.matched_text:
Data Processing and MNPI Handling Addendum between Qinhuai Digital Services Co., Ltd. (USCC: 92321000MA9X7Y321P) and Fenriver Asset Management (Beijing) Co., Ltd. (USCC: 91110108MA7C2R45QX) for PRC operations.
This Addendum governs Personal Information under the PIPL and non-public information under the PRC Securities Law as part of cloud hosting and transaction analytics services.
Processor shall process PI only for portfolio onboarding, issuer due diligence, employment HR workflows, and cyber incident response, in accordance with PIPL, the Cybersecurity Law, the Data Security Law, and internal information-barrier policies.
Categories include names, work emails, role titles, PRC resident ID numbers (e.g., 32**************17, fictional example), device identifiers, and employees’ dependent data; sensitive PI for minors under 14 requires separate guardian consent.
MNPI may arise from draft token-issuance whitepapers for a virtual asset pilot and pre-disclosure ESG/climate audit findings relating to an issuer; such MNPI is segregated with access strictly need-to-know.
Controller shall provide insider lists for live mandates and enforce blackout windows around board approvals and exchange filing dates; Processor maintains tamper-evident logs and prohibits tipping or selective disclosure.
We operate physical and logical information barriers between research analytics and client coverage teams, with segregated collaboration spaces for deal-stage workstreams and a ban on personal devices in the clean room.
Unannounced cyber incident indicators (e.g., a zero-day exploitation of the Fenriver VPN) are treated as MNPI until public disclosure and handled under the Cybersecurity Law; simulated incidents in training are educational only and not tied to any live client environment.
Pricing schedules, draft term sheets for a carbon-credit tokenization POC, and supplier SLAs are Confidential Information and may constitute inside information where linked to an issuer’s material transactions.
Cross-border transfers (e.g., support access from our Singapore operations center) require separate PIPL consent, security assessment by the competent PRC cyberspace regulator where thresholds are met, and standard contracts; data localization applies to designated critical information infrastructure.
Retention: MNPI repositories are retained for 6 years from mandate close; HR records for 10 years per statutory archiving; cyber logs for 12 months unless legal hold applies; upon expiry we anonymize or delete and record the action.
Data subjects (employees, candidates, and client contacts) may request access, correction, or deletion; where legal retention obligations override, we will explain the legal basis and partially fulfill the request.
Please contact Ms. Liang Yanyan, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@qinhuai-digital.example.cn or +86-21-4000-8899-01 for PIPL requests and MNPI handling queries.
Sub-processors include Zhongbei Cloud Compute Co., Ltd. (USCC: 92500000MA0K3L8Z9T) for PRC hosting; we will give 30 days’ notice of changes and maintain an information barrier between managed services and advisory teams.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
